citations.description: test only asserts plan_review_result_v1, 0.6, 0.7 — missing .criteria/.decision/.validity and recording-location checks
doc content does contain all required elements; the gap is the test not asserting them

findings.criterion: The criterion ships advisory (0.95) with fire-rate observable in REVIEW_RESULT sidecars. In-session proof (a COMMITTED repo file, CI-verifiable): the committed file `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md` exists AND contains the promotion-decision PATH — the fire-rate/validity jq query (referencing the `plan_review_result_v1` schema and the `.criteria`/`.decision`/`.validity` fields), the literal '0.6' and '0.7' band bounds, and the recording location — proven by a unit test (tests/) that asserts the file exists AND greps it for those anchors (content check, not mere existence).
findings.detail: The document `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md` exists and its content satisfies all the named requirements (contains the jq query referencing `plan_review_result_v1`, `.criteria`, `.decision`, `.validity`; contains `0.6` and `0.7`; names the recording location as follow-up ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d). However, the unit test (`tests/unit/test_plan_review_g7.py::test_g7_promotion_path_doc_exists_and_documents_the_path`, lines 98–106) only explicitly asserts `plan_review_result_v1`, `0.6`, and `0.7`. It does NOT grep for `.criteria`, `.decision`, `.validity` as anchor strings, nor does it assert the presence of the 'recording location' (the follow-up ticket reference). The AC explicitly requires the test to grep for those specific anchors — the test only partially satisfies that obligation.
findings.remediation: Add assertions to the test for `.criteria`, `.decision`, `.validity` (or the containing jq expression) and for the recording-location reference (e.g. `72b6` or the text naming where the promotion decision will be recorded).

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
